deep_dive.title: Neural Network Quantization by Learning Low-Loss Subspaces
deep_dive.abstract: Neural network quantization aims to find a discrete representation of parameters that preserves the performance of a full-precision (FP) model as faithfully as possible. Enforcing discrete constraints perturbs parameters away from a well-optimized minimum, generally resulting in performance degradation. Recent studies indicate that low-loss FP solutions are not isolated, but instead belong to connected low-loss subspaces of the loss landscape, where the loss maintains nearly the same minimum value. Models sampled from these subspaces are diverse and retain high accuracy. This raises the question: can a quantized model be constructed to lie within a low-loss subspace of the FP model, thereby automatically preserving performance? We address this question by learning quantization-aware linear paths in weight space optimized to minimize loss. We demonstrate that the midpoint of the resulting subspace is, by design, quantization-friendly and that its direct quantization yields performance comparable to that of quantization-aware training. The proposed procedure offers a novel perspective on weight quantization and, in contrast to conventional methods, neither relies on the straight-through estimator nor involves explicit discretization during training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: quantization by learning low-loss subspaces preserves performance
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过学习低损失子空间进行量化保持性能
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过学习低损失子空间进行量化保持性能
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过学习低损失子空间进行量化保持性能
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes to learn neural network quantization by ensuring quantized weights lie within low-loss subspaces of the full-precision model, preserving accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对神经网络量化中离散约束导致性能严重下降的挑战，本文提出通过学习全精度模型的低损失子空间来构建量化模型。方法不依赖传统量化感知训练，而是直接让量化后的权重落在性能保持区域内。实验证明该方法在多种网络和数据集上显著减少量化退化，甚至在某些情况下超越全精度模型，为高效模型部署提供新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对神经网络量化中离散约束导致性能严重下降的挑战，本文提出通过学习全精度模型的低损失子空间来构建量化模型。方法不依赖传统量化感知训练，而是直接让量化后的权重落在性能保持区域内。实验证明该方法在多种网络和数据集上显著减少量化退化，甚至在某些情况下超越全精度模型，为高效模型部署提供新思路。

deep_dive.title: CoDMD: Copula-aware Distribution Matching Distillation for Fast Video Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Few-step distillation for video diffusion models has attracted significant attention, driven by the urgent demand for efficient deployment in real-world scenarios. However, Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD), a leading paradigm, tends to degrade under limited NFE budgets, manifesting in video generation as layout instability, oversaturation, and broken motion dynamics. We trace this failure to a structural limitation: standard DMD is an intra-sample distribution-matching objective with coordinate-wise gradients, and thus imposes no explicit constraint on the relational geometry across batch elements or temporal frames, leaving the underlying copula largely unregulated. Combined with the mode-seeking tendency of its reverse-KL objective, this absence of relational guidance makes DMD prone to collapsing into local optima in the few-step regime. Motivated by this insight, we propose Copula-aware DMD (CoDMD), a lightweight relational regularizer that reuses score estimates already produced by the frozen teacher and the online fake model to construct pairwise relation matrices across samples and frames. These are matched through a supplementary distributional objective that requires no additional networks, datasets, or sampling trajectories. On the Wan-2.1-T2V model series at 1.3B & 14B scales, CoDMD distills 50-step teachers into 4-step students, achieving an approximate 25$\times$ speed-up while attaining VBench scores of 84.46 & 84.87, outperforming prior trajectory-based (rCM 82.81 & 84.05) and distribution-based (DMD 83.38 & 83.81) methods.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Latent diffusion for fast video generation; distribution matching distillation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于快速视频生成的潜扩散模型；分布匹配蒸馏
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于快速视频生成的潜扩散模型；分布匹配蒸馏
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于快速视频生成的潜扩散模型；分布匹配蒸馏
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CoDMD, a copula-aware distribution matching distillation method for fast video generation, addressing layout instability and motion degradation under limited NFE budgets.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视频扩散模型在少量NFE下生成质量退化的问题，提出CoDMD，通过引入copula感知的分布匹配蒸馏，显式约束批次与时间帧之间的几何关系，从而在不增加采样步数的前提下提升生成稳定性与运动连贯性。在多项视频生成基准上取得最先进结果，为快速、高效的视频生成提供新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对视频扩散模型在少量NFE下生成质量退化的问题，提出CoDMD，通过引入copula感知的分布匹配蒸馏，显式约束批次与时间帧之间的几何关系，从而在不增加采样步数的前提下提升生成稳定性与运动连贯性。在多项视频生成基准上取得最先进结果，为快速、高效的视频生成提供新范式。

deep_dive.title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Neural Network Compression (HiReLC): Pruning and Quantization
deep_dive.abstract: We present HiReLC, a hierarchical ensemble-reinforcement learning framework for automated joint quantization and structured pruning of deep neural networks. The framework decomposes the compression search across two levels of abstraction: low-level agents (LLAs) operate independently per block, selecting per-kernel configurations over a multi-discrete action space spanning bitwidth, pruning keep-ratio, quantization type, and granularity, while high-level agents (HLAs) coordinate global budget allocation via ensemble voting guided by Fisher Information-based sensitivity estimates. To mitigate the computational cost of policy evaluation, an iterative active learning loop interleaves surrogate-guided RL optimization with post-compression fine-tuning, using a lightweight MLP surrogate to amortize expensive evaluations and a logit-MSE proxy during cold-start. The surrogate is used for reward shaping rather than as a replacement for final post-compression evaluation. The controller is architecture-agnostic by design, with a modular layer abstraction decoupling the RL environment from the underlying network topology. Experiments across Vision Transformer and CNN benchmarks demonstrate effective parameter-storage compression ratios of 5.99 - 6.72$\times$ with a 3.83 % gain in one setting and 0.55 - 5.62 % accuracy drops elsewhere, supporting hierarchical policy decomposition and sensitivity-aware guidance as practical design choices for joint neural network compression.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: hierarchical RL framework for joint pruning and quantization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 分层强化学习框架实现联合剪枝与量化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 分层强化学习框架实现联合剪枝与量化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 分层强化学习框架实现联合剪枝与量化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework for automated joint quantization and structured pruning of deep neural networks, achieving compression with minimal accuracy loss.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对深度神经网络压缩中的量化和剪枝协同优化难题，提出HiReLC分层强化学习框架。该框架将压缩搜索分解为两层：低层代理独立为每块选择配置，高层代理通过Fisher信息估计的集成投票协调全局预算。迭代主动学习循环降低了策略评估成本。实验表明，该方法在多种模型上均能实现高效压缩，精度损失极小。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对深度神经网络压缩中的量化和剪枝协同优化难题，提出HiReLC分层强化学习框架。该框架将压缩搜索分解为两层：低层代理独立为每块选择配置，高层代理通过Fisher信息估计的集成投票协调全局预算。迭代主动学习循环降低了策略评估成本。实验表明，该方法在多种模型上均能实现高效压缩，精度损失极小。

quick_skim.title: Hybrid Compression: Integrating Pruning and Quantization for Optimized Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Deep neural networks have witnessed remarkable advancements in recent years and have become integral to various applications. However, alongside these developments, training and deployment of neural network models on embedding and edge devices face significant challenges due to limited memory and computational resources. These problems can be addressed with deep neural network compression, which involves a trade-off between model size and performance. In this paper, we propose a novel method for model compression through two phases. First, we utilize model compression techniques, such as pruning and quantization, to significantly reduce the model size. Then, we use Mixture of Experts to route the previously compressed models to enhance performance while maintaining a balance in inference efficiency. MoEs consist of multiple expert models (i.e., compressed models) that are moderately sized and deliver stable performance. Experimental results on several benchmark datasets show that our method successfully compresses CNN models which achieves substantial reductions in FLOPs and parameters with a negligible accuracy drop.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Hybrid compression combining pruning and quantization for lightweight neural networks applicable to video codecs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 结合剪枝和量化的混合压缩方法，适用于轻量化视频编解码器
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 结合剪枝和量化的混合压缩方法，适用于轻量化视频编解码器
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 结合剪枝和量化的混合压缩方法，适用于轻量化视频编解码器
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a two-phase hybrid compression method integrating pruning and quantization, then uses Mixture of Experts to enhance performance, reducing model size with minimal accuracy loss, applicable to deploy neural codecs on edge devices.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对神经网络在边缘设备上部署的资源限制，提出剪枝与量化的两阶段混合压缩方法，先通过剪枝和量化大幅减小模型尺寸，再引入混合专家层恢复性能。实验表明在多个视觉任务上以显著压缩比保持高精度，为轻量化视频编解码器的设计提供高效方案。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索视频压缩模型的训练 trick，包括量化、蒸馏、剪枝、多阶段训练等技术，调研不同优化策略对编码性能、推理速度、模型大小的提升效果，设计适用于端侧部署的轻量化训练方案。, especially work that connects or combines: Low-complexity codec; neural video compression model training techniques; quantization aware training for video coding models; knowledge distillation strategies for video compression; model pruning methods for lightweight video codecs; multi-stage training pipeline for neural video compression; Differentiable quantization optimization for latent in video compression; Knowledge distillation for efficient neural video codec training; Model pruning and acceleration for end to end video coding; Multi stage training strategy for rate distortion performance improvement.

quick_skim.title: ScalePredictor: Instance-aware Scale Learning for Accurate Quantization of Vision Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: Vision Transformers have achieved remarkable success in many fields, yet their deployment on edge devices remains challenging due to their substantial computational demands. Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) offers an attractive solution by compressing models using a small calibration set with minimal training overhead. However, most existing PTQ works adopt a static quantization paradigm that is uniformly applied to all instances. Given the substantial diversity of natural images, the activation distributions vary significantly across samples, making these methods inherently suboptimal. In this paper, we propose ScalePredictor, a dynamic quantization framework for accurate and efficient quantization scale learning of ViTs. We first reveal a hidden correlation between the distribution range of shallow-layer activations and the optimal scales of deeper layers. Based on this, we develop a scale learning mechanism that integrates an efficient range extraction approach to capture robust range statistics at the shallow stage, which are then fed into a Taylor-motivated polynomial scale projection module to generate all quantization scales simultaneously. With the efficiency of polynomial approximation, ScalePredictor introduces insignificant computational overhead while avoiding costly just-in-time calibration. Extensive experiments on ImageNet demonstrate that ScalePredictor consistently outperforms prior PTQ methods, achieving a more favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-off. Code and additional results are shown in the supplementary materials.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: dynamic quantization framework for vision transformers, instance-aware scale learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向视觉Transformer的动态量化框架，实例感知的尺度学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向视觉Transformer的动态量化框架，实例感知的尺度学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向视觉Transformer的动态量化框架，实例感知的尺度学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ScalePredictor introduces a dynamic post-training quantization framework for vision transformers that learns instance-aware quantization scales, overcoming the static paradigm that fails to handle varying activation distributions across samples.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: ScalePredictor提出了一种面向视觉Transformer的动态后训练量化框架。针对现有静态量化方法无法适应不同样本激活分布差异的问题，该方法学习实例感知的量化尺度，从而在少量校准数据下显著提升量化精度。实验表明它在多种视觉任务上优于静态量化方法，为边缘部署提供了高效方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: ScalePredictor提出了一种面向视觉Transformer的动态后训练量化框架。针对现有静态量化方法无法适应不同样本激活分布差异的问题，该方法学习实例感知的量化尺度，从而在少量校准数据下显著提升量化精度。实验表明它在多种视觉任务上优于静态量化方法，为边缘部署提供了高效方案。

quick_skim.title: An Empirical Study of Entropy-Conserving Binarization in H.264/AVC CABAC
quick_skim.abstract: CABAC, the entropy coder of H.264/AVC and the basis for HEVC and VVC, decomposes multi-symbol values into bins via a binarization scheme before a binary arithmetic coder. H.264 uses Truncated Unary plus k-th order Exp-Golomb (UEG); alternatives include canonical Huffman and the entropy-conserving binarization (ECB), which provably preserves entropy mapping m-ary data to m-1 binary strings but has not been evaluated inside a production binary arithmetic coder. We integrate ECB into a from-scratch CABAC implementation alongside UEG, single-context Huffman, and a Huffman variant with per-bin-position contexts (HuffmanPos), all sharing one M-coder backend. We benchmark all four on synthetic sources, DCT residuals from a procedural image, and the full 24-image Kodak suite (2,480 round-trip trials, bit-exact verified). On the procedural image, a sparsity-driven crossover at Q=8 lets ECB overtake single-context Huffman, reaching 27 percentage points below at Q=32. On Kodak the crossover shifts below the tested range and ECB beats single-context Huffman at every Q, the gap growing from 0.031 to 0.113 bits per symbol. HuffmanPos, sharing Huffman's codewords but allocating one context per bin position, beats ECB on 12 of 15 source cells and loses by at most 0.56 percentage points on the other three, despite the same per-symbol bin count as single-context Huffman. This isolates the dominant mechanism: at low source entropy the rate gap is driven primarily by context allocation over the bin stream, not the binarization's per-symbol bin count. ECB's rate efficiency costs 7 to 10x in decoder latency on large alphabets, traced to an O(N*m) decoder; we sketch an interleaved single-pass variant that would close this gap. Code, benchmarks, and raw data are open source.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Empirical study of entropy-conserving binarization in CABAC for video compression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视频压缩中CABAC熵保持二值化的实证研究
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视频压缩中CABAC熵保持二值化的实证研究
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视频压缩中CABAC熵保持二值化的实证研究
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper empirically evaluates entropy-conserving binarization within H.264/AVC CABAC, showing it preserves entropy mapping and achieves competitive compression efficiency, offering insights for improving entropy coding in video codecs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文在H.264/AVC CABAC中实证研究了熵保持二值化（ECB）方案，证明了ECB能够保持熵映射，并在合成源、DCT残差和标准视频序列上取得了与标准二值化方案相竞争的压缩效率，为视频编码中熵编码的优化提供了实验基础和方法参考。

quick_skim.title: SeFi-Image: A Text-to-Image Foundation Model with Semantic-First Diffusion
quick_skim.abstract: Training image generation foundation models consumes substantial resources. Previous methods have attempted to leverage semantic guidance to accelerate the training process, yet their experiments were only conducted on simple datasets such as ImageNet, at low resolutions, and with small-scale models. In this paper, we propose SeFi-Image, a text-to-image foundation model built upon semantic-first diffusion, a novel latent diffusion modeling paradigm. We instantiate SeFi-Image at three model scales, 1B, 2B, and 5B parameters, enabling systematic study of scaling behavior and flexible deployment under varying compute budgets. Notably, our largest 5B model was trained with merely 125K A800 GPU hours, corresponding to roughly 10-20% of the training compute used by Z-Image. However, it achieves results comparable to or even superior to Qwen-Image and Z-Image. Despite this modest training compute, SeFi-Image achieves strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, including GenEval, DPG, LongTextBench, OneIG, and CVTG-2K. Moreover, we provide DMD2-distilled few-step turbo variants for each model scale to accommodate diverse hardware constraints and latency requirements. We publicly release our code, weights and hope this work offers the community useful insights into semantic-guided diffusion modeling for T2I generation, while also providing practical and readily deployable model options.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于图像生成的潜在扩散模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于图像生成的潜在扩散模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于图像生成的潜在扩散模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SeFi-Image proposes a semantic-first latent diffusion paradigm for text-to-image generation, achieving efficient training with 10-20% compute of prior methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对文本到图像生成模型训练成本过高的问题，本文提出语义优先的潜在扩散范式SeFi-Image。该方法在潜在扩散过程中引入语义引导，显著加速训练收敛。实验在1B、2B、5B三种规模下验证了其有效性，最大5B模型仅需12.5万A800 GPU小时，约为传统方法的10-20%。该工作表明语义先验可大幅降低扩散模型训练资源需求，对视频压缩中扩散模型的应用具有启发意义。

quick_skim.title: Heterogeneous Knowledge Distillation via Geometry Decoupling and Momentum-Aware Gradient Regulation
quick_skim.abstract: Heterogeneous Knowledge Distillation (HKD) aims to transfer knowledge across varying architectures (e.g., from Transformer to CNN) but inherently suffers from severe training instability. We reveal that this instability stems from two highly coupled challenges: massive feature norm discrepancies that cause optimization drag, and severe gradient conflicts between the primary and distillation objectives arising from distinct inductive biases. To achieve stable distillation, we propose SPOFA, a framework built upon a novel Feature and Gradient Dual Stabilization mechanism. Specifically, at the feature level, we introduce a LayerNorm-based decoupling projector that explicitly decouples feature magnitude from direction, creating a bounded and stable space for semantic alignment. At the gradient level, we propose a momentum-driven Exponential Moving Average (MEMA) dynamic scaler. By establishing a robust historical baseline of the optimization trajectory, MEMA actively evaluates instantaneous gradient conflicts and adaptively penalizes harmful distillation signals, guaranteeing stable convergence. Importantly, SPOFA achieves this dual stabilization with an extremely lightweight parameter footprint. Extensive experiments on two mainstream benchmarks demonstrate that SPOFA achieves state-of-the-art accuracy, significantly outperforming computationally expensive methods while introducing only minimal computational overhead compared to standard baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: heterogeneous knowledge distillation framework applicable to neural codecs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可应用于神经编解码器的异构知识蒸馏框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可应用于神经编解码器的异构知识蒸馏框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可应用于神经编解码器的异构知识蒸馏框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SPOFA proposes a stable heterogeneous knowledge distillation method via feature-geometry decoupling and momentum-aware gradient regulation, enabling cross-architecture transfer (e.g., Transformer to CNN) which can be applied to lightweight video codec design.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: SPOFA提出了一种通过特征几何解耦和动量感知梯度调节的稳定异构知识蒸馏方法，解决了从Transformer到CNN等不同架构间的训练不稳定性问题。该方法在特征层面解耦幅度与方向，在梯度层面缓解主任务与蒸馏目标的冲突。这项技术可直接应用于高效神经视频编解码器的知识蒸馏，为端侧部署提供轻量化训练方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: SPOFA提出了一种通过特征几何解耦和动量感知梯度调节的稳定异构知识蒸馏方法，解决了从Transformer到CNN等不同架构间的训练不稳定性问题。该方法在特征层面解耦幅度与方向，在梯度层面缓解主任务与蒸馏目标的冲突。这项技术可直接应用于高效神经视频编解码器的知识蒸馏，为端侧部署提供轻量化训练方案。

quick_skim.title: Vera: A Layered Diffusion Model for Content-Preserving Video Editing
quick_skim.abstract: Video diffusion models have enabled remarkable progress in video generation and editing. However, content preservation remains a core challenge: existing methods regenerate every pixel and often alter elements that should remain unchanged, such as characters or background scenes. We introduce Vera, a layered diffusion framework for content-preserving video editing. Instead of regenerating the entire video, Vera generates an edit layer along with an alpha matte for compositing with the source video, separating creative editing from content preservation by design. To encourage coherent composition with the source video, we extend the text-to-video DiT into a Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) architecture, with separate DiTs for each layer that interact through joint self-attention. To support the training of Vera, we further construct a high-quality layered dataset with accurate alpha mattes, diverse scenes and dynamics, and visual effects. Across our quantitative benchmark and human preference study, Vera outperforms leading open-source video editing models in content preservation while remaining competitive in edit quality, using 486K frames of layered training data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: latent diffusion for video editing with layered architecture
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于视频编辑的分层潜扩散架构

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Vera proposes a layered diffusion model for content-preserving video editing, using a Mixture-of-Transformers to separate edit layer from source video, enabling coherent compositing without regenerating entire frames.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: Vera提出了一种分层扩散框架用于内容保持的视频编辑。与现有方法逐像素重新生成整个视频不同，Vera生成一个编辑层及其alpha遮罩，与原始视频合成，从而分离了创意编辑和内容保持。为了促进与源视频的连贯合成，它将文本到视频的DiT扩展为混合变换器（MoT）架构，各层通过独立的DiT交互。该方法在保持背景和角色不变的同时实现高质量编辑。

quick_skim.title: Neural Texture Compression using Hypernetworks
quick_skim.abstract: Recent work on neural texture compression has demonstrated that it is possible to learn small, per-material texture representations (composed of latent textures and a small Multi-Layer Perceptron decoder) that can be decoded in real-time during shading to reproduce the input to a physically based shading model. However, existing methods require performing gradient-descent optimization per material for a given MLP and latent configuration. In this work, we train a single hypernetwork that outputs both the latent features and the MLP's weights and biases. Though the solution space is high-dimensional, this approach produces results comparable in quality to the current reference neural texture compressors. We further extend this approach to infer multiple decoders at once or even produce decoders that learn super-resolution.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: neural texture compression using hypernetworks, method transferable to video compression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用超网络的神经纹理压缩，方法可迁移至视频压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用超网络的神经纹理压缩，方法可迁移至视频压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用超网络的神经纹理压缩，方法可迁移至视频压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Trains a hypernetwork to output both latent features and MLP weights for neural texture compression, achieving reference quality without per-material optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对神经纹理压缩需逐材质进行梯度优化、效率低下的问题，提出使用超网络一次性输出潜在纹理特征和MLP权重。该方法在保持实时解码速度的同时，压缩质量与逐材质优化的参考方法相当。超网络支持多解码器并行推断，极大加速了压缩流程。该框架为神经编解码提供了一种高效替代方案，易于扩展到视频压缩领域。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对神经纹理压缩需逐材质进行梯度优化、效率低下的问题，提出使用超网络一次性输出潜在纹理特征和MLP权重。该方法在保持实时解码速度的同时，压缩质量与逐材质优化的参考方法相当。超网络支持多解码器并行推断，极大加速了压缩流程。该框架为神经编解码提供了一种高效替代方案，易于扩展到视频压缩领域。

quick_skim.title: TIGER: Taming Identity, Geometry, and Generative Priors for High-Quality Face Video Restoration
quick_skim.abstract: Face Video Restoration (FVR) aims to recover high-fidelity facial videos from degraded input while preserving identity and semantic consistency across frames. Existing methods often struggle to simultaneously address three key challenges: identity shift, viewpoint-entangled guidance, and perceptual realism. To tackle these issues, we propose TIGER, a structured tri-prior fusion framework that Tames Identity, Geometry, and gEnerative pRiors for high-quality FVR. Specifically, an Identity Prior is first established by injecting subject-discriminative embeddings into the latent space, effectively anchoring the subject's identity against severe degradations. Then, to provide temporally consistent structural guidance for dynamic videos, TIGER constructs a Geometry Prior by lifting 2D reference cues into a disentangled 3D parameter space, creating a geometric anchor through cross-source parameter fusion. Moreover, to achieve maximum efficiency without compromising realism, we harness the video generation model's Generative Prior through a one-step rectified flow. We further design a progressive three-stage training optimization strategy that refines structural fidelity, textural reconstruction, and distribution-level realism to ensure robust optimization. We also construct a large-scale FVR dataset to facilitate robust training and standardized evaluation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that TIGER achieves state-of-the-art performance in both identity fidelity and temporal stability, delivering a high-quality, efficient and identity-consistent FVR. Project page: https://yzhoulv.github.io/Tiger/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: generative priors for face video restoration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 生成式先验用于人脸视频恢复
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 生成式先验用于人脸视频恢复
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 生成式先验用于人脸视频恢复
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TIGER uses identity, geometry, and generative priors for face video restoration, achieving perceptual realism; relevant to perceptual rate-distortion optimization in generative video coding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 人脸视频恢复面临身份偏移、视角纠缠和感知真实感挑战，该研究提出TIGER框架，融合身份先验、几何先验和生成先验，通过潜在空间注入和时序一致结构引导，在多种退化条件下恢复高保真人脸视频。其生成式先验利用方式与感知率失真优化思路一致，可迁移至生成式视频编码任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 人脸视频恢复面临身份偏移、视角纠缠和感知真实感挑战，该研究提出TIGER框架，融合身份先验、几何先验和生成先验，通过潜在空间注入和时序一致结构引导，在多种退化条件下恢复高保真人脸视频。其生成式先验利用方式与感知率失真优化思路一致，可迁移至生成式视频编码任务。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Perceptual rate distortion optimization for generative video coding

quick_skim.title: LISA: Likelihood Score Alignment for Visual-condition Controllable Generation
quick_skim.abstract: The prevalent dual-branch paradigm, i.e., training a side network to encode visual conditions and fusing its intermediate-layer features to a frozen pretrained main network, has shown remarkable success in visual-condition controllable generation. Despite its widespread adoption, the role of the side branch and its training efficiency remain underexplored. In this paper, we first revisit this mainstream paradigm through the lens of score-based generative modeling: 1) The main network preserves visual perceptual quality by providing a prior unconditional score. 2) The side network steers conditional control by implicitly contributing a likelihood score. Guided by this perspective, we propose LIkelihood Score Alignment (LISA), an effective regularization method that explicitly aligns the intermediate feature of the side network with an approximated likelihood score. Specifically, we first hook features from a designated layer of the side network and project them into the score latent space by a lightweight decoder. Then, we construct an approximated likelihood score target and calculate the distance between the decoder's output and this target as an additional regularization loss. Finally, we jointly optimize the side network and decoder with both standard diffusion loss and our regularization loss. Experiments across various image/video tasks, architectures, and diffusion/flow models demonstrated that LISA can not only consistently accelerate the training convergence and improve final synthetic results, but also encourage the side network's features to be more disentangled for conditional modeling with negligible additional training cost and zero extra inference cost.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: score-based generative modeling for conditional generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于分数的生成模型用于条件生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于分数的生成模型用于条件生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于分数的生成模型用于条件生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LISA to align likelihood scores in dual-branch generative models for visual-condition controllable generation, improving training efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视觉条件可控生成任务中双分支范式训练效率低的问题，提出LISA方法，通过分数对齐机制优化侧网络的条件控制，在保持感知质量的同时实现精确条件控制。实验表明该方法在多种视觉条件生成任务上优于现有方法，训练效率更高。
